problem_spec.summary: Given a prime $p$ and integers $0\le k\le n$, compute simultaneously (i) the exponent $e=v_p\!\left(\binom{n}{k}\right)$ and (ii) the residue $r\equiv\binom{n}{k}\pmod p$, using base-$p$ expansions (Lucas theorem / carry counting) and factorial valuation formulas.
ask: Output the pair $(e,r)$ where $e=v_p\!\left(\binom{n}{k}\right)$ and $r\equiv\binom{n}{k}\pmod p$ with $0\le r<p$.

parameter(s).name: p
parameter(s).purpose: sets the base for digit expansions and the valuation

parameter(s).name: n
parameter(s).purpose: top index of the binomial coefficient

parameter(s).name: k
parameter(s).purpose: bottom index; controls carry structure in base $p$

parameter(s).name: variant
parameter(s).purpose: extends to related combinatorial divisibility tasks while keeping the same base-$p$ interface

invariant(s).name: base_p_digit_interface
invariant(s).statement: Write $n=\sum n_i p^i$ and $k=\sum k_i p^i$ with digits $0\le n_i,k_i<p$. The digit data $(n_i,k_i)$ drive both Lucas (mod $p$ residue) and Kummer/Legendre (valuation via carries).

invariant(s).name: carry_count_equals_vp
invariant(s).statement: (Optional/Kummer) $v_p\!\left(\binom{n}{k}\right)$ equals the number of carries when adding $k$ and $n-k$ in base $p$.

essential_assumption(s): Digits are taken in base $p$; the instance must keep $p$ prime to use Lucas/Kummer in their standard forms.
When outputting a residue $r$, normalization is $0\le r<p$ (structural).
uniqueness_certificate(s): Both $e=v_p\!\left(\binom{n}{k}\right)$ and the reduced residue $r\in\{0,\dots,p-1\}$ are uniquely determined by $n,k,p$; thus the pair $(e,r)$ is globally unique.

technique(s).name: Kummer_theorem_optional
technique(s).purpose: (optional) compute $e$ via carry counting rather than floor sums
outputs: interpretation of $e$ as number of carries

theorem(s).name: combinatorial_count_as_integer
theorem(s).role: justify integrality of expressions by counting interpretations (often used to prove divisibility)

closest_first_principle_concept_applied: Base-$p$ expansions expose how factorials accumulate prime powers and how binomial coefficients decompose digitwise modulo $p$.

critical_trick(s): Use the shared interface "base-$p$ digits of $n$ and $k$": Lucas gives the mod $p$ residue and Kummer/Legendre gives the $p$-adic valuation.
For sum-of-binomial divisibility variants, interpret the sum as counting objects modulo $p$ or track valuation term-by-term.

library(s).name: math
library(s).reason: Core integer arithmetic: gcd, lcm, modular exponentiation scaffolding.

library(s).name: sympy
library(s).reason: Optional symbolic/number theory utilities: factorint, primerange, mod_inverse, crt; useful for verification tasks.

import_hint(s): from sympy import factorint, crt, mod_inverse

task(s).name: primary_structural_check
task(s).reason: Validate that the produced object(s) satisfy all defining congruences/divisibility/Diophantine constraints for randomly sampled parameters within the declared domain.

task(s).name: secondary_independent_check
task(s).reason: Cross-check the main result via an alternative computation (e.g., brute force for small bounds, or an independent formula such as factorization vs. valuation, or CRT reconstruction vs. direct search).

task(s).name: lucas_vs_bruteforce_small_n
task(s).reason: For small $n,k,p$, compute $\binom{n}{k}$ exactly and compare both $e$ and $r$ to Lucas/Legendre outputs.

task(s).name: carry_count_check_optional
task(s).reason: Implement base-$p$ addition carry counting and verify it matches $e=v_p\binom{n}{k}$ for random small inputs.
